name: Downtown 1 — Hostage Standoff (original)
_notes: FIRST FULLY-ORIGINAL LEVEL — uses ZERO extracted SEGA assets. No "baseStage", so the engine builds the procedural downtown (scene/OriginalEnvironment.js) instead of loading any stage GLB; enemies are the procedural humanoids; the camera follows this authored railPath in CameraRig curve mode (no camera.bin). Theme: a fictional precinct response to a downtown hostage standoff (real events as inspiration only — names/places invented). Wave/enemy semantics: each "position" is a CAMERA-RELATIVE offset resolved at spawn (x = right, -z = ahead, y = 0 → dropped onto the street). "time" is seconds of gameplay along the rail; a matching "clearPoints" entry HALTS the camera there until the wave is cleared (an arena). Keep enemy x within ±5 (street half-width 6) and z around -12..-22 for face-to-face range. type: grunt (hp1), gunman (hp2, shoots harder), heavy (hp4), innocent (hostage/civilian — do NOT shoot, costs a life).
